perturbation.original_text: EFCA shall: 3.1.1 immediately upon re ceipt of a written reques t from the disclos ing part, return to the disc losing part y all doc uments and materials (and all copies thereof) containing t he disclos ing party’s Confidential Information and certify in writing to the dis closing par ty that it has complied with the requirements of this sub-clause; and 3.1.2 notwithstanding the completi on of any purpose for which any Confidential Informati on has been disclosed or the return of documents and materials as aforesaid, continue to the bound by t he undertakings set out i n clauses 2, 4 and 5.
perturbation.changed_text: EFCA shall, upon written request, return all materials containing the disclosing party's information.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'and all copies thereof' from clause 3.1.1, the contract no longer explicitly requires EFCA to return all copies of confidential information. This omission contradicts the previous section where it was stated that 'Copies may be made as needed', making the obligation to return all materials containing the disclosing party's information unclear and unenforceable regarding copies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: notwithstanding the completi on of any purpose for which any Confidential Informati on has been disclosed or the return of documents and materials as aforesaid, continue to be bound by the undertakings set out i n clauses 2, 4 and 5.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that 'all documents and materials (and all copies thereof)' must be returned. The changed text omits the phrase 'and all copies thereof,' creating a direct contradiction with the original obligation. The contradicted text continues to bind EFCA to undertakings even after the return of documents, which implies that everything, including copies, must be returned to trigger this continuation of obligations. By removing the explicit requirement to return copies, the changed text creates a conflict in the contract, making it unclear whether EFCA is still bound by clauses 2, 4, and 5 if it does not return all copies. Therefore, it's a strong in-text contradiction, aligning with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Limitations and Warrant y 4.1 EFCA shall: 4.1.1 not divulge the di sclosing party’s Confidenti al Information, in whole or in part, to any third party; and 4.1.2 make no c ommercial use of t he same or any par t thereof without the prior wr itten consent of the di sclosing party. No twithstanding the 2 foregoing, EFCA shall be entitled to make any disc losure required by law of t he disc losing party’s Confide ntial Information provide d that it gives the disc losing party not less t han two business days’ notice of such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Limitations and Warranty 4.1 EFCA shall: 4.1.1 not divulge the disclosing party’s Confidential Information to any third party without prior written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'in whole or in part', the changed text now allows EFCA to potentially divulge parts of the Confidential Information to third parties without explicit consent, contradicting the intention of complete confidentiality. Also, removing the part where states 'EFCA shall be entitled to make any disc losure required by law of t he disc losing party’s Confide ntial Information provide d that it gives the disc losing party not less t han two business days’ notice of such disclosure' introduces a direct conflict with legal obligations. EFCA can not make disclosures required by law because it doesn't have the option to do so, even with a 2 day prior notice, therefore it can get into a legal issue because it doesn't comply with the law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The disc losing party reserves all ri ghts in its Confidential Information and no rights or obligations other t han those expressly recited herein are granted or to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, no licenc e is hereby granted directly or ind irectly under any patent, invention, discovery, copyright or ot her industrial property right now or in the future held, made, obtained or licen sable by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the allowance for disclosures required by law, which creates a direct conflict with the legal obligations that EFCA has to comply with. This directly contradicts the original agreement where the disclosing party reserves all rights in its Confidential Information, since EFCA can no longer comply with the law and could potentially get into a legal issue.

perturbation.original_text: Confidentiality EFCA agr ees to keep the existenc e and nature of this Agreement confidential and not to use the same or the name of the disclos ing party (or of any company in the Group of Companies of which the disclosing party forms part) in any public ity, adv ertisement or other disclosure with regard to this Agreement without the pr ior written consent of the disclos ing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidentiality EFCA agrees to keep the core tenets of this Agreement confidential. Disclosure of the Agreement's nature is permitted for legal and financial compliance purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The edited clause states, 'Disclosure of the Agreement's nature is permitted for legal and financial compliance purposes.' This allowance directly contradicts the original statement that 'EFCA agrees to keep the existence and nature of this Agreement confidential.' The ambiguity of 'core tenets' adds uncertainty, as it is undefined what aspects of the agreement can or cannot be disclosed, leading to an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement and under standing between the parties in respect of C onfidentia l Information and supersedes all previous agreement s, understandings and undertakings in suc h respect. This Agreement cannot be c hanged except by wr itten agreement between t he parties. The inter pretation, construction and effect of this Agreement shall be enforced in accor danc e with the laws of the State of Victoria. This agree ment will b e effective as of the date below, and contin ue for five (5) years.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates complete confidentiality, whereas the changed text allows disclosure for legal and financial compliance, contradicting the original intent. This change conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that the agreement constitutes the 'entire agreement' and cannot be changed except by written agreement between the parties, therefore the change in text created contradiction as it changed the agreement without written consent.

perturbation.original_text: Entire Agreement, Governing La w and Jurisdiction This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement and under standing between the parties in respect of C onfidentia l Information and supersedes all previous agreement s, understandings and undertakings in suc h respect. This Agreement cannot be c hanged except by wr itten agreement between t he parties. The inter pretation, construction and effect of this Agreement shall be enforced in accor danc e with the laws of the State of Victoria. This agree ment will b e effective as of the date below, and contin ue for five (5) years.
perturbation.changed_text: Entire Agreement, Governing Law and Jurisdiction This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties. The interpretation, construction and effect of this Agreement shall be enforced in accordance with general principles of equity.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall be enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of Victoria' to 'shall be enforced in accordance with general principles of equity', the clause creates a conflict. Removing the reference to the laws of Victoria means disputes are no longer bound by a specific legal framework, conflicting with the initial agreement that all aspects should be confidential and handled under a defined legal system. The new addition can be seen as too ambiguous because its general and doesn't point to anything specific.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No variatio n, alteration or modifi cation of this Agree ment will be valid unless in writing s igned by a duly authorised representative of both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies enforcement according to the laws of Victoria, while the changed text dictates enforcement according to general principles of equity. This removes the specific legal framework. The contradicted text states that any modification must be in writing signed by both parties. The change from 'laws of Victoria' to 'general principles of equity' fundamentally alters the enforcement mechanism of the agreement, and since this change was not made in writing signed by both parties as per the contradicted section, a contradiction exists. The removal of a specific legal framework creates ambiguity and contradicts the requirement for written and signed modifications.
